description: The negation case. Reviewer triggers are POSITIVE globs only — there is no `!`-negation syntax in a trigger list. The negation the router supports is 'no trigger matched', which skips the reviewer. This changeset is docs-only (a README + a markdown guide) and matches NO reviewer's triggers, so every reviewer is skipped and `fires` is empty. NOTE: this fixture appeared flaky for a while and was briefly quarantined — that was a red herring. The command reliably produced an empty `fires:` line all along; the bug was in the grader (check_routing.py), whose `fires:` regex used `\s*` after the colon and so swallowed the newline on an EMPTY fires line, capturing the next (`skips:`) line as the fired set. Fixed by matching `[ \t]*` instead. The universal-negative (empty fires) is the only case that exposed it.
